⭐ Health and Physical Education

Minor, Local and Ball Games

Discover fun traditional games and exciting ball games from around the world!

📚 Unit 8 ⏱️ 11 min read

Unit 8: Minor, Local and Ball Games

Games are fun! They keep us active, teach teamwork, and help us make friends. This unit covers traditional local games and popular ball games.

Lesson 1: Minor and Local Games

What are Local Games?

Local games (also called traditional or indigenous games) are games that have been played in a region for many generations. They often use simple equipment or no equipment at all.

Importance of Local Games

ImportanceExplanation
Preserve cultureKeep traditions alive
No expensive equipmentPlay with anything available
Develop skillsRunning, jumping, throwing, teamwork
Build communityChildren and adults play together
Outdoor activityFresh air and exercise

Popular Local Games of Nepal and South Asia

1. Kabaddi

FeatureDescription
Players7 per team
EquipmentNone
How to playA “raider” enters opponent’s half, holds breath, chants “kabaddi”, and tries to touch opponents
ScoringTouch opponents and return to your half

Basic rules:

  • Raider must hold breath and chant continuously
  • Defenders try to catch the raider
  • If caught, the raider is out

2. Kho-Kho

FeatureDescription
Players9 per team
EquipmentNone (just a field)
How to playChasers sit in a row; runners run around
ScoringChaser touches runner

Basic rules:

  • 3 runners try to avoid 9 chasers
  • Chasers sit in a line facing opposite directions
  • “Kho” is shouted to give a chaser permission to run

3. Dandi Biyo (Gilli Danda)

FeatureDescription
EquipmentTwo sticks: long (danda) and short (gilli)
How to playHit the gilli with the danda; run before it is caught
Similar toCricket or baseball

Basic rules:

  • Place gilli on a small stone
  • Hit the gilli with the danda
  • Run to a point and back while the opponent catches the gilli

4. Lagori (Seven Stones / Pittu Garam)

FeatureDescription
Equipment7 flat stones and a soft ball
How to playKnock down the stone pile with a ball; rebuild before being hit

Basic rules:

  • Two teams: hitters and fielders
  • Hitters knock down the pile
  • Fielders try to hit hitters with the ball
  • Hitters rebuild the pile to score

5. Marbles (Goti / Kancher Guti)

FeatureDescription
EquipmentGlass marbles
How to playShoot your marble to hit opponent’s marble

Basic rules:

  • Draw a circle
  • Place marbles inside
  • Use your marble to knock others out
  • Keep the marbles you knock out

6. Hopscotch (Kit Kit / Stapu)

FeatureDescription
EquipmentChalk (to draw squares) and a small stone
How to playHop on one foot through the squares, push a stone

Basic rules:

  • Draw a pattern of squares on the ground
  • Throw a stone into a square
  • Hop through the squares without stepping on lines

7. Rope Skipping (Jump Rope)

FeatureDescription
EquipmentRope
How to playJump over a rope swung by yourself or two people

Types:

  • Single rope (alone)
  • Double rope (two people turning)
  • Rhymes and songs with jumps

8. Hide and Seek

FeatureDescription
Players3 or more
How to playOne person counts while others hide; find all hiders

Basic rules:

  • “Den” is a safe spot
  • The seeker counts to a number (e.g., 50)
  • Hiders try to reach the den without being caught

9. Tag (Chain Tag / Freeze Tag)

FeatureDescription
PlayersAny number
How to playOne person is “it” and chases others

Variations:

  • Chain tag: Caught players join hands to catch others
  • Freeze tag: Caught players freeze until unfrozen by another player
  • Shadow tag: Step on shadows instead of touching

10. Sack Race

FeatureDescription
EquipmentPotato sack or pillowcase
How to playPut both legs in a sack and hop to the finish line

Basic rules:

  • Keep both hands on the sack
  • Do not fall
  • First to cross finish line wins

Benefits of Playing Local Games

BenefitExplanation
Physical fitnessRunning, jumping, hopping
Social skillsTeamwork, communication
Problem solvingStrategy and quick thinking
No screen timeOutdoor play
InclusiveAnyone can play, regardless of skill

Lesson 2: Ball Games

What are Ball Games?

Ball games are sports played with a ball. They are played all over the world.

Popular Ball Games

1. Football (Soccer)

FeatureDescription
Players11 per team
EquipmentRound ball (size 5)
ObjectiveScore goals by kicking ball into opponent’s net
RulesNo hands except goalkeeper
Duration90 minutes (two halves of 45 minutes)

Basic skills:

  • Passing (short and long)
  • Dribbling (moving with the ball)
  • Shooting (kicking toward goal)
  • Tackling (taking the ball from opponent)
  • Heading (using head to hit the ball)

Positions:

PositionRole
GoalkeeperStop goals (only player who can use hands)
DefenderStop opponent from scoring
MidfielderLink defense and attack
Forward/StrikerScore goals

💡 Famous player: Lionel Messi, Cristiano Ronaldo

2. Basketball

FeatureDescription
Players5 per team
EquipmentOrange ball, hoop at 10 feet
ObjectiveShoot ball into opponent’s hoop
RulesMust dribble (bounce) while moving

Basic skills:

  • Dribbling (bouncing the ball)
  • Passing (chest pass, bounce pass)
  • Shooting (layup, jump shot)
  • Rebounding (catching missed shots)

Scoring:

ShotPoints
Free throw1 point
Field goal (inside arc)2 points
Three-pointer (outside arc)3 points

💡 Famous player: Michael Jordan, LeBron James

3. Volleyball

FeatureDescription
Players6 per team
EquipmentNet, lightweight ball
ObjectiveHit ball over net so opponent cannot return it
RulesMaximum 3 touches per side

Basic skills:

  • Serve (underhand or overhand)
  • Pass (bump/forearm pass)
  • Set (overhead pass for attacker)
  • Spike (hard hit downward)
  • Block (jump at net to stop spike)

Rotations: Players rotate positions clockwise after winning the serve.

💡 Famous player: Karch Kiraly, Giba

4. Cricket

FeatureDescription
Players11 per team
EquipmentBat, ball (hard or tennis), wickets
ObjectiveScore more runs than opponent

Basic rules (simple version):

  • Two teams: batting and bowling
  • Bowler bowls the ball
  • Batter hits the ball and runs between wickets
  • Fielders try to get the batter out

Ways to get out:

MethodExplanation
BowledBall hits the wicket
CaughtFielder catches hit ball before it bounces
Run outWicket is broken while batter is running
LBWBall hits leg in front of wicket

Scoring:

RunDescription
1, 2, 3 runsBatter runs between wickets
4 runsBall bounces and goes to boundary
6 runsBall goes over boundary without bouncing

💡 Famous player: Sachin Tendulkar, Virat Kohli

5. Throwball

FeatureDescription
Players7 per team
EquipmentNet, lightweight ball
ObjectiveThrow ball over net so opponent cannot catch it

Basic rules:

  • Catch the ball with both hands
  • Throw within 5 seconds
  • No dribbling or stepping with the ball
  • Ball must be thrown from above shoulder level

Popular in: South Asia (especially India and Nepal)

6. Handball

FeatureDescription
Players7 per team (6 court players + 1 goalkeeper)
EquipmentSmall ball, goals
ObjectiveThrow ball into opponent’s goal

Basic rules:

  • Players can run with ball (max 3 steps)
  • No touching the ball with feet
  • Goalkeeper is the only player who can touch ball with feet

Rules Common to Most Ball Games

RuleExplanation
Fair playNo pushing, hitting, or cheating
Respect officialsFollow referee/umpire decisions
Stay in boundsBall out of play = turnover
No double touchCannot touch ball twice in a row (except in some games)
TeamworkPass to teammates, do not be selfish

Health Benefits of Playing Games

BenefitExplanation
Cardiovascular healthRunning improves heart and lungs
Strong bones and musclesJumping, throwing, kicking
CoordinationHand-eye and foot-eye coordination
Weight controlBurns calories
Mental healthReduces stress, makes you happy
Social skillsMakes friends, learns teamwork
DisciplineFollows rules and respects others

Try It Yourself!

Exercise 1: Fill in the Blanks

  1. Kabaddi and Kho-Kho are examples of ______ games.
  2. In football, the goalkeeper is the only player who can use their ______.
  3. In basketball, you must ______ the ball while moving.
  4. In cricket, hitting the ball over the boundary without bouncing gives ______ runs.
  5. The game of ______ is played with a net and players use bump, set, and spike.

Exercise 2: Match the Game to Its Equipment

GameEquipment
Kabaddia) Bat and ball
Volleyballb) None
Cricketc) Net and ball
Hopscotchd) Chalk and stone

Exercise 3: True or False

  1. In football, players can use their hands anywhere on the field. (T/F)
  2. Kho-Kho has a raider who chants. (T/F)
  3. In basketball, a shot from outside the arc is worth 3 points. (T/F)
  4. Throwball is played with a bat. (T/F)
  5. Local games often need expensive equipment. (T/F)

Exercise 4: Name the Game

Read the description and name the game.

  1. Seven stones, a ball, and two teams → ______
  2. Two sticks: one long, one short → ______
  3. Hopping in sacks to a finish line → ______
  4. Players sit in a row and chase runners → ______

Answer Key

Exercise 1: 1. local/traditional, 2. hands, 3. dribble, 4. six, 5. volleyball

Exercise 2: Kabaddi→b, Volleyball→c, Cricket→a, Hopscotch→d

Exercise 3: 1. F, 2. F, 3. T, 4. F, 5. F

Exercise 4: 1. Lagori/Seven stones, 2. Dandi Biyo/Gilli Danda, 3. Sack race, 4. Kho-Kho

Great work! Games are for fun, friendship, and fitness. Play every day!
